entry.title: Ρεκόρ τετραετίας στις τιμές πετρελαίου εν μέσω νέων αμερικανικών σχεδίων για το Ιράν
entry.summary: Brent crude topped $126 a barrel, the highest since Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine, after the White House signaled a multi-month blockade of Iranian ports and CENTCOM drafted plans for 'short, sharp' strikes. President Trump met top oil and energy executives at the White House to discuss domestic output, Venezuela, futures contracts, gas and shipping logistics.

entry.title: Axios: Σχέδια για νέες επιθέσεις στα χέρια του Τραμπ – Τα σενάρια
entry.summary: Axios reports that CENTCOM commander Adm. Brad Cooper will brief President Trump today on options for a 'short, sharp' new wave of strikes against Iran — potentially targeting infrastructure to break the negotiating stalemate. Two other options: a partial seizure of the Strait of Hormuz, possibly with ground forces, and a special-operations raid on Iran's enriched uranium stockpiles.

entry.title: Μοτζτάμπα Χαμενεΐ: «Λαμπρό» το μέλλον για τον Περσικό Κόλπο χωρίς την αμερικανική παρουσία
entry.summary: Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, declared that the Persian Gulf's 'brilliant future' will be 'without America,' vowing Tehran will secure the Strait of Hormuz and end 'enemy abuses.' He framed the region's emerging order as a regional partnership and called Iran's nuclear and ballistic capabilities 'national assets' to be defended.

entry.title: ΗΠΑ – Ιράν: O «τζόγος» του Τραμπ με τον ναυτικό αποκλεισμό
entry.summary: A CNN analysis dissects Trump's gamble on a naval blockade of Iran. The bet is that economic suffocation will force Tehran's hand: unemployment near 1 million, soaring inflation, even red meat out of reach. But the Islamic Republic has weathered decades of sanctions and an eight-year war with Iraq. Analysts warn the strategy may yield neither collapse nor surrender — and that political will at home may waver before Tehran does.

entry.title: Ενόχληση Αγκυρας για Ελλάδα – Γαλλία: Κάθε συμμαχία κατά της Τουρκίας θα αποτύχει
entry.summary: Ankara reacted angrily to Macron's pledge in Athens that France would stand by Greece if its sovereignty is threatened. The Turkish defense ministry warned that any 'military alliance against Turkey' is doomed to fail and questioned a possible French troop deployment in Cyprus, accusing Paris of fueling regional tensions.

entry.title: Το παρασκήνιο του ρήγματος στο καρτέλ πετρελαίου
entry.summary: The UAE has formally left OPEC, deepening its rift with Saudi Arabia. National oil firm ADNOC says it can lift output from 3.4 million barrels a day to 5 million once freed from quotas, even as Hormuz disruptions have pushed UAE output down 44% to 1.9 million b/d. OPEC's combined production has fallen 27% since the war began. The move tightens UAE-Washington alignment under Trump.

entry.title: Γιατί οι Αμερικανοί ετοιμάζονται να παίξουν το «χαρτί» του Dark Eagle;
entry.summary: The U.S. is preparing to deploy the Dark Eagle hypersonic missile (Long Range Hypersonic Weapon) in the Middle East theater. With a range above 2,700 km, it could strike deep inside Iran without risking aircraft or crews. Each round costs about $15 million; only eight have been built. The first battery, run by the 17th Field Artillery, received its initial operational assessment on March 20, 2026.

entry.title: Αξιωματούχος Πενταγώνου: Στα 25 δισ. δολάρια το κόστος του πολέμου στο Ιράν
entry.summary: A senior Pentagon official told the House Armed Services Committee that the Iran war has cost $25 billion so far — Washington's first official estimate, with most of it spent on munitions. War Secretary Pete Hegseth defended the figure ('What would you give to keep Iran from a bomb?') and lashed Democratic critics as 'reckless and defeatist' six months out from midterms in which the war is testing Republican support.

entry.title: Μητσοτάκης στα εγκαίνια του Πανεπιστημίου Λευκωσίας στην Αθήνα: Σημαντικό βήμα, επόμενο η αναθεώρηση του άρθρου 16
entry.summary: PM Mitsotakis inaugurated the Athens branch of the University of Nicosia alongside Cypriot President Christodoulides. He defended the law allowing foreign university branches in Greece and announced an upcoming constitutional revision of Article 16 to permit private universities, aiming to make Greece a regional and global education hub.

entry.title: Η Ουκρανία βάζει όρους στη χρήση των drones
entry.summary: Talks between Greece and Ukraine on co-producing maritime drones (USVs) have hit a snag: Kyiv wants veto power over how Greek armed forces use them, which Athens rejects. Greek officials see it as Ukraine balancing its relationship with Turkey. Discussions continue on transferring some production to Greek shipyards and gaining access to battle-tested Ukrainian tech.

entry.title: Ευχαριστίες από τον εμίρη του Κατάρ στον Μητσοτάκη
entry.summary: The emir of Qatar Tamim bin Hamad al Thani thanked PM Mitsotakis for Greece's stance during the Iran war that began Feb 28. Athens supported Saudi and UAE air defense, working closely with Washington and Paris. Greece now seeks to capitalize through expanded military cooperation and Qatari investments, with both Trump and Macron publicly praising Greece's allied conduct.

entry.title: H Eυρώπη έχει πρόβλημα: Δεν γνωρίζει πόσα καύσιμα της απομένουν
entry.summary: Per a Politico investigation, the EU lacks a clear picture of fuel reserves across its 27 member states amid the Middle East war. No mandatory reporting mechanism exists. Greece has gone as far as asking the Commission to set up a WhatsApp/Signal channel between capitals. Brussels has now floated a 'Fuel Observatory' to monitor production, imports, exports and stocks.

entry.title: Μάχη με τον χρόνο για το «Σπίτι μου ΙΙ»
entry.summary: About 3,500 prospective borrowers are racing to make a tightened June 2 deadline (brought forward from August 30) for the 'My Home II' subsidized mortgage scheme. Only 350-400 have signed contracts; another 3,100 risk being shut out. Total budget is €2 billion with €1.6 billion approved; 10,000 households have benefited so far. The Finance Ministry is exploring a bridge program for stragglers.

entry.title: Ενεργειακή κρίση: Αλιεία, θαλάσσιες και οδικές μεταφορές πλήττονται περισσότερο
entry.summary: The European Commission approved a temporary state-aid framework for industries hammered by the Middle East energy shock. The Commission flags acute risk of business closures in fishing, maritime and road transport, rail freight, and primary agriculture; fertilizer prices have jumped 61% over 2024 levels and diesel rose 21% year-on-year in March 2026 — issues felt sharply in Greece's island-dependent shipping.

entry.title: «Κατεβάζει» την ανάπτυξη και «ανεβάζει» τον πληθωρισμό στην Ελλάδα η Fitch
entry.summary: Fitch Solutions cut Greece's 2026 GDP forecast to 1.9% (from 2.2%) and lifted its inflation forecast to 3% (from 2.5%) on energy-cost spillovers from the war. Greece's heavy 38% reliance on natural gas for power leaves it exposed; Fitch puts the probability of further conflict escalation hitting energy infrastructure at 45%, while still expecting fixed investment to grow 7.2%.

entry.title: Επίθεση σε ΕΦΚΑ και Πρωτοδικείο: Στα δικαστήρια ο 89χρονος για την απολογία του
entry.summary: The 89-year-old who attacked the EFKA pension office and the Athens Court of First Instance on April 28 appeared before an investigating magistrate. He faces three felonies, including attempted multiple homicide of five staff. Through his lawyer, he admits he carefully planned the attack but denies intent to kill — claiming he aimed 'below the knees' out of despair after his social-security claim was repeatedly denied.

entry.title: Αυστηρά μηνύματα ευρωπαϊκών θεσμών προς την Αγκυρα για κράτος δικαίου και δημοκρατία
entry.summary: Senior European officials sent a sharply worded joint letter to Turkey's interior minister Mustafa Çiftçi warning over the country's democratic backsliding. Signatories — including PACE rapporteurs and an MEP rapporteur for Turkey — single out the two-year pretrial detention of Istanbul mayor Ekrem İmamoğlu and other elected opposition figures as incompatible with the European Convention on Human Rights.

entry.title: Μαρινάκης: Επιπόλαιη η πρόταση Ανδρουλάκη για 4ήμερη εργασία, θα οδηγούσε σε «λουκέτα»
entry.summary: Government spokesperson Pavlos Marinakis dismissed PASOK leader Androulakis's proposal for a mandatory four-day workweek as 'superficial and populist,' warning it would force small businesses to close, raise unemployment and push prices higher. He noted 95% of Greek firms employ up to nine staff and could not absorb the cost. The government already allows an optional four-day arrangement by mutual agreement.

entry.title: «Συν» και «πλην» Ανδρουλάκη – Τσίπρα στη μάχη για τη δεύτερη θέση
entry.summary: Five pollsters and political scientists weigh the Androulakis-vs-Tsipras battle for second place. Androulakis appeals to centrist voters but is dragged by his governing past, while Tsipras benefits from frustration on the progressive flank but carries heavy negatives. With Mitsotakis as a fixed pole, the contest is intensely personality-driven.

entry.title: Εντός και Εκτός: Στον Σκαραμαγκά η αναβάθμιση των υποβρυχίων
entry.summary: The 'In and Out' column reports: Turkey plans a gas pipeline to occupied northern Cyprus and is restructuring special forces brigades in western Turkey citing 'possible tension with Greece'; former minister Evangelos Venizelos will be made an honorary doctor at the University of Athens; and TKMS and the Skaramangas shipyards signed an exclusive deal to upgrade the Hellenic Navy's four Type-214 submarines.
